heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by John Schlesinger. Written by Waldo Salt, based on the novel of the same name by James Leo Herlihy.
clean: Directed by John Schlesinger. Written by Waldo Salt, based on the novel of the same name by James Leo Herlihy.

heading: Joe Buck
raw: You know what you can do with them dishes. And if you ain't man enough to do it for yourself, I'd be happy to oblige. I really would.
clean: You know what you can do with them dishes. And if you ain't man enough to do it for yourself, I'd be happy to oblige. I really would.

heading: Joe Buck
raw: Lotta rich women back there, Ralph, begging for it, paying for it, too...and the men - they're mostly tutti fruttis. So I'm gonna cash in on some of that, right?...Hell, what do I got to stay around here for? I got places to go, right?

raw: Well, sir, I ain't a for-real cowboy. But I am one helluva stud.

heading: Joe Buck

heading: Joe Buck

heading: Enrico Salvatore "Ratso" "Rico" Rizzo
raw: [to Joe] Terrific shirt.

heading: Enrico Salvatore "Ratso" "Rico" Rizzo

heading: Enrico Salvatore "Ratso" "Rico" Rizzo

heading: Enrico Salvatore "Ratso" "Rico" Rizzo

heading: Enrico Salvatore "Ratso" "Rico" Rizzo
raw: You know, in my own place my name ain't Ratso. I mean, it just so happens that in my own place, my name is Enrico Salvatore Rizzo...At least call me Rico in my own god-damn place.

heading: Enrico Salvatore "Ratso" "Rico" Rizzo
raw: Not bad, not bad for a cowboy. You're OK. You're OK.

heading: Enrico Salvatore "Ratso" "Rico" Rizzo
raw: You want the word on that brother and sister act. Hansel's a fag, and Gretel's got the hots for herself so who cares, right?

heading: Enrico Salvatore "Ratso" "Rico" Rizzo

heading: Others

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ratso: I gotta get outta here, gotta get outta here. Miami Beach, that's where you could score. Anybody can score there, even you. In New York, no rich lady with any class at all buys that cowboy crap anymore. They're laughin' at you on the street.
